goal: Infer strong harness defaults first, then confirm only the decisions that materially change the generated repo-local workflow.

decisionPoints.title: Project Identity
questions: Project name
Developer name
One-line product brief

decisionPoints.title: Plan Selection
questions: Choose core stack family A-F
If sidecar/runtime preset is needed: choose G/H/I/J/K

decisionPoints.title: Delivery Constraints
questions: Team size
MVP scope and acceptance criteria

decisionPoints.title: Runtime Defaults
questions: Confirm inferred package manager
Permission mode: Permissionless / Plan-only / Standard (default: Plan-only)
Confirm commit policy

decisionPoints.title: Webapp Rendering Model
questions: Choose webapp rendering/deployment model: none, client-only, start-workers, astro-content, or custom
If the app needs SEO/SSR plus an authenticated workspace, prefer start-workers with one apps/web frontend, / SSR/public route, and /app client-only boundary

decisionPoints.title: AI-Native Profile
questions: Choose AI-native overlay: none (default), chat-agent, collaborative-editor, runtime-console, product-copilot, workflow-agent, generative-ui-agent, browser-agent, research-agent, coding-agent, enterprise-agent-platform, voice-agent, or sidecar-kernel
Confirm whether generated structure should add AG-UI, assistant-ui or CopilotKit, Bun/Hono gateway, MCP/tool, sidecar, and observability boundaries

decisionPoints.title: Orchestration Profile
questions: Confirm planner -> generator -> evaluator harness flow
Override artifact strictness only if the team needs a lighter loop

decisionPoints.title: Evaluation Profile
questions: Choose browser QA vs command QA default
Any extra scoring dimensions or hard thresholds

decisionPoints.title: Context Profile
questions: Choose context profile: stable-root + progressive-subdir (default) or flat-root
Confirm whether paired CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md files should be generated only for primary functional blocks in multi-module repos

decisionPoints.title: Documentation Profile
questions: Use minimal-agentic docs by default
Generate optional product, stack, decision, architecture, and package docs only when evidence exists

decisionPoints.title: Handoff Profile
questions: When should the repo force a handoff artifact
Any custom handoff sections or restore expectations

decisionPoints.title: Recovery & State Profile
questions: Choose recovery policy: lesson-driven (default) or structured-guards
Choose state policy: file-backed (default) or context-light

decisionPoints.title: Quality Rules
questions: Project-specific prohibitions
Any must-enforce compliance constraints

decisionPoints.title: External Tooling
questions: Use layered routing for complex/gstack, simple/Waza, and knowledge/gbrain workflows
Default host coverage: Claude Code + Codex
Enable gbrain knowledge-layer guidance while leaving MCP disabled by default

decisionPoints.title: Plugin Profile
questions: Install recommended global plugins
Enable LSP set by stack

shared-long-running-harness.label: Shared long-running harness
shared-long-running-harness.description: Default planner -> generator -> evaluator loop with repo-local artifacts and strict execution gates.

lightweight-collaboration.label: Lightweight collaboration
lightweight-collaboration.description: Keeps the artifact model but uses softer review and handoff gates.

browser-qa.label: Browser QA + evaluator rubric
browser-qa.description: Use browser-first QA evidence and UI-aware scoring when the plan includes a user-facing surface.

command-qa.label: Command QA + evaluator rubric
command-qa.description: Use command/test/build evidence and reliability scoring when the plan is backend or TUI focused.

artifact-aware.label: Artifact-aware handoff
artifact-aware.description: Force a structured handoff artifact on context rollover, failed verification, and session stop.

manual-handoff.label: Manual handoff
manual-handoff.description: Generate handoff artifacts on demand while keeping the same file format.

lesson-driven.label: Lesson-driven
lesson-driven.description: Keep human-readable recovery prompts and use tasks/lessons.md as the primary improvement loop.

structured-guards.label: Structured guards
structured-guards.description: Emit standardized failure_class metadata from guards while keeping the same human-readable recovery text.

hybrid.label: Hybrid
hybrid.description: Combine structured guard metadata with lesson-driven recovery and automatic handoff refresh.

file-backed.label: File-backed
file-backed.description: Persist execution, verification, and handoff state to repo-local artifacts.

context-light.label: Context-light
context-light.description: Use the same workflow surface with fewer persisted state assumptions for lightweight repos.

stable-root-progressive-subdir.label: Stable root + progressive subdir
stable-root-progressive-subdir.description: Keep the root contract small and expose paired CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md files for primary functional blocks through a context map.

flat-root.label: Flat root
flat-root.description: Keep a single root context surface for smaller repos that do not need functional-block context files.

none.label: None
none.description: Use the selected A-K family without a webapp rendering/deployment overlay.

client-only.label: Client-only Vite
client-only.description: Dense interactive app, dashboard, internal tool, or embedded surface that does not need crawler-visible landing HTML.
client-only.deployment: Cloudflare Pages/static assets or Worker static assets by explicit deploy target
client-only.publicRoute: Client-rendered routes only; no SEO SSR guarantee
client-only.fallback: Use start-workers when public SEO/SSR matters
techStackRows: | Webapp model | Client-only Vite + TanStack Router/Query |
| Deploy | Static assets via Pages or Worker assets by explicit target |
| SEO boundary | No SSR guarantee; use Plan C start-workers for crawler-visible landing HTML |

start-workers.label: TanStack Start on Cloudflare Workers
start-workers.description: Single React webapp deployment for public SEO/SSR landing plus authenticated or browser-heavy workspace routes.
start-workers.deployment: Cloudflare Workers with @cloudflare/vite-plugin, wrangler.jsonc, Worker assets, and wrangler deploy
start-workers.publicRoute: `/` SSR/prerender-capable landing route with meta, OG, canonical, and crawler-visible HTML
start-workers.appRoute: `/app` client-only route boundary via route-level `ssr: false`; lazy-load browser-only/WebGL components
start-workers.fallback: If Start + Workers fails a thin gate, evaluate Vike or React Router Framework Mode; do not split into two default frontends
techStackRows: | Webapp model | TanStack Start + Vite + React |
| Deploy | Cloudflare Workers, `wrangler.jsonc`, Worker assets, `wrangler deploy` |
| Public route | `/` SSR/prerender-capable landing with meta/OG/canonical |
| Workspace route | `/app` client-only with route-level `ssr: false` and lazy browser-only components |
| Frontend count | One deployable frontend component under `apps/web`; backend Workers remain separate only when they own API/Agent/MCP runtime authority |

astro-content.label: Astro content shell
astro-content.description: Content-heavy site, docs, blog, or mostly-static landing shell with islands where interaction is limited.
astro-content.frontend: Astro + TypeScript + React islands only when needed
astro-content.deployment: Cloudflare Pages/Workers or Node adapter selected by SSR/runtime needs
astro-content.appRoute: Separate explicit app surface only when required, not an implicit second frontend
astro-content.fallback: Use start-workers when the React app itself needs both SEO/SSR landing and authenticated workspace
techStackRows: | Webapp model | Astro content/SSR shell |
| App boundary | Add a React app surface explicitly only when interaction justifies it |
| SEO boundary | Content-first SSR; not the default dense React workspace shell |

custom.label: Custom
custom.description: Manually specified web rendering and deploy model.
custom.fallback: Record the tradeoff and verification gate in the plan
techStackRows: | Webapp model | Custom, explicitly documented |

none.label: None
none.description: Use the selected A-K plan without an AI-native app runtime overlay.

chat-agent.label: Chat agent
chat-agent.description: Ordinary AI chat, RAG, or help assistant inside the selected app plan.
chat-agent.frontend: Vite 8 + assistant-ui or the selected plan UI
chat-agent.runtimeProtocol: AI SDK UI stream or AG-UI lite
chat-agent.backend: Bun/Hono gateway or Mastra/Hono runtime behind the existing API boundary
chat-agent.sidecarPolicy: Python only when the model framework needs it
techStackRows: | AI UI | assistant-ui chat surface |
| Agent stream | AI SDK UI stream or AG-UI lite |
| Agent runtime | Bun/Hono by default; Mastra when TS agents need memory, tools, workflows, or evals |
| Agent state | TanStack Query + Zustand |

collaborative-editor.label: Collaborative editor
collaborative-editor.description: AI-aware document, CMS, or knowledge workspace with local-first collaboration and explicit sync ownership.
collaborative-editor.runtimeProtocol: Loro CRDT document events; AI SDK or AG-UI only for user-visible agent runs
collaborative-editor.backend: Bun/Hono sync and agent gateway, or existing API boundary
collaborative-editor.stateDefault: Loro document state + TanStack Query server cache + Zustand UI state
collaborative-editor.sidecarPolicy: None by default; Python or Go sidecars only for extraction, indexing, or heavy sync workers
collaborative-editor.uiSchema: Plate node/plugin schema plus app-owned document contracts
techStackRows: | Editor UI | Plate + shadcn/ui in a Vite 8 React surface |
| Collaboration | Loro CRDT document state and sync contracts |
| Agent runtime | AI SDK or AG-UI only for user-visible assistant/document actions |
| Sync gateway | Bun/Hono or existing API boundary owns persistence and auth |
| State split | Loro for document replicas; TanStack Query for server cache; Zustand for UI chrome |

runtime-console.label: Runtime console
runtime-console.description: Agent runtime console with trace, replay, prompt playground, approvals, artifacts, and tool-call visibility.
runtime-console.frontend: Vite 8 + TanStack Router + assistant-ui + trace/timeline panels
runtime-console.sidecarPolicy: Python sidecar allowed for model framework or eval pipelines
runtime-console.uiSchema: A2UI optional experiment, not production default
techStackRows: | AI UI | assistant-ui + custom run console |
| Agent protocol | AG-UI events over HTTP streaming |
| Agent gateway | Bun/Hono `/api/agent/run` |
| Agent state | TanStack Query + Zustand + Jotai |
| Contracts | Zod/Valibot run, event, tool, approval, artifact schemas |
| Observability | OpenTelemetry spans/log events for model, tool, approval, and sidecar latency |

product-copilot.label: Product copilot
product-copilot.description: SaaS in-app copilot that can inspect product context and call business actions through narrow tools.
product-copilot.sidecarPolicy: MCP tools for business actions; sidecars only by capability
techStackRows: | Copilot UI | CopilotKit or assistant-ui headless |
| Agent protocol | AG-UI app-domain events |
| Business tools | MCP or narrow HTTP actions |
| Agent state | TanStack Query + Zustand/Jotai |

workflow-agent.label: Workflow agent
workflow-agent.description: Visual workflow or DAG builder for composing agent steps and tool execution.
workflow-agent.sidecarPolicy: Mastra, LangGraph, Temporal, Inngest, or BullMQ only when execution needs workflow durability or orchestration
techStackRows: | Workflow UI | React Flow/xyflow + Monaco |
| Agent protocol | AG-UI workflow/run events |
| Executor | Bun/Hono gateway with optional Mastra or durable workflow engine |
| Agent state | Jotai + Zustand + TanStack Query |

generative-ui-agent.label: Generative UI agent
generative-ui-agent.description: Agent-generated forms, cards, tables, and artifacts through a safe component registry.
generative-ui-agent.sidecarPolicy: Only when renderer or tool kernels need it
techStackRows: | Generated UI | Safe React registry |
| Agent protocol | AG-UI events carrying app-domain payloads |
| UI schema | A2UI experimental, not executable code |
| Agent state | Jotai + TanStack Query |

browser-agent.label: Browser agent
browser-agent.description: Browser automation, computer-use, or RPA workbench with explicit task/run visibility.
techStackRows: | Browser console | Vite 8 custom run console |
| Agent protocol | AG-UI browser-run events |
| Automation worker | Playwright/Browserbase/Stagehand sidecar |
| Agent state | Jotai + TanStack Query |

research-agent.label: Research agent
research-agent.description: Evidence and report workspace for deep research, artifacts, citations, and handoffs.
techStackRows: | Research UI | assistant-ui + artifact workspace |
| Agent protocol | AG-UI evidence and artifact events |
| Research backend | Bun/Hono gateway with optional Python pipeline |
| Agent state | TanStack Query + Zustand + Jotai |

coding-agent.label: Coding agent
coding-agent.description: AI IDE, repo, PR, or DevOps agent with diff, terminal, and sandbox surfaces.
coding-agent.sidecarPolicy: Sandbox worker, Go, or Rust optional by capability
techStackRows: | Coding UI | assistant-ui + Monaco + diff/terminal panels |
| Agent protocol | AG-UI with optional MCP tool boundary |
| Sandbox | Worker or sidecar boundary |
| Agent state | Jotai + TanStack Query |

enterprise-agent-platform.label: Enterprise agent platform
enterprise-agent-platform.description: Multi-tenant platform with shared agent gateway, capability-scoped sidecars, and docs/marketing shell.
enterprise-agent-platform.frontend: Astro for web/docs/SSR shell + Vite 8 apps
techStackRows: | Web shell | Astro docs/marketing + Vite 8 app surfaces |
| Agent protocol | AG-UI platform events |
| Agent gateway | Bun/Hono multi-tenant boundary |
| Capability sidecars | MCP/HTTP per capability |

voice-agent.label: Voice agent
voice-agent.description: Realtime voice or call assistant with media and run state boundaries.
techStackRows: | Voice UI | Vite 8 realtime controls |
| Agent protocol | AG-UI side-channel + WebRTC |
| Media boundary | Realtime/media sidecar optional |
| Agent state | TanStack Query + Zustand |

sidecar-kernel.label: Sidecar kernel
sidecar-kernel.description: TypeScript app core with Python, Go, or Rust kernels behind MCP or narrow HTTP boundaries.
sidecar-kernel.frontend: Selected plan frontend; Vite 8 app shell when interactive
sidecar-kernel.runtimeProtocol: AG-UI when user-visible agent runs exist; MCP/HTTP for sidecars
sidecar-kernel.stateDefault: TanStack Query + Zustand, plus domain stores as needed
sidecar-kernel.sidecarPolicy: Python ADK/FastAPI for model/eval/data pipelines, Go/Gin for high-concurrency workers or infra adapters, Rust for low-latency parsing/indexing/sandboxing
techStackRows: | App gateway | Bun/Hono owns app-facing API |
| Sidecar protocol | MCP tools or narrow HTTP jobs |
| Python sidecar | Google ADK/FastAPI, model frameworks, evals, data science |
| Go sidecar | Gin workers, Supabase REST adapters, high-concurrency infra jobs |
| Rust sidecar | Parsing, indexing, sandboxing, native kernels |
